mnemonics.title: Double Brackets for Direct Access
mnemonics.content: REMEMBER: DOUBLE BRACKETS [[ ]] GIVE THE CONTENT, SINGLE BRACKETS [ ] GIVE A SUBCONTAINER. THINK OF [[ ]] AS UNWRAPPING THE GIFT, WHILE [ ] IS JUST LOOKING AT THE WRAPPED BOX.

mnemonics.title: Lists vs Vectors
mnemonics.content: LISTS ARE RECURSIVE (CAN NEST ANYTHING), VECTORS ARE ATOMIC (MUST BE SAME TYPE). LIKE A ROOM (LIST) THAT CAN HOLD FURNITURE, APPLIANCES, AND EVEN OTHER ROOMS, WHILE A DRAWER (VECTOR) CAN ONLY HOLD ONE TYPE OF ITEM.

mnemonics.title: lapply vs sapply
mnemonics.content: LAPPLY = LIST APPLY = ALWAYS RETURNS LIST. SAPPLY = SIMPLIFYING APPLY = TRIES TO SIMPLIFY. REMEMBER: L FOR LIST, S FOR SIMPLIFY.

mnemonics.title: NULL Removes Elements
mnemonics.content: SETTING TO NULL IS LIKE THROWING AWAY THE ELEMENT COMPLETELY. MY_LIST[[N]] <- NULL = REMOVE THAT SLOT FROM THE LIST.

acronyms.expansion: LIST IS SEQUENTIAL TYPES - R DATA STRUCTURE HOLDING ORDERED COLLECTION OF OBJECTS
acronyms.usage: REMEMBER THAT LISTS CAN HOLD DIFFERENT TYPES - THE 'S' IN SEQUENTIAL STANDS FOR STORING MULTIPLE DATA TYPES
APPLY FUNCTION TO EACH ELEMENT AND RETURN LIST
APPLY FUNCTION AND TRY TO SIMPLIFY RESULT TO VECTOR OR MATRIX

visualTips.visualization: THINK OF A LIST LIKE A FILING CABINET WITH LABELED FOLDERS. EACH FOLDER CAN CONTAIN DIFFERENT THINGS - SOME MAY HAVE NUMBERS, SOME LETTERS, SOME MAY EVEN HAVE OTHER CABINETS. THE FOLDERS ARE THE ELEMENTS, AND THE CABINET IS THE LIST.
SINGLE BRACKET [ ] = PHOTO OF THE BOX (STILL A LIST). DOUBLE BRACKET [[ ]] = OPENING THE BOX AND TAKING OUT THE ITEM. THE $ OPERATOR IS LIKE A LABEL ON THE BOX YOU CAN DIRECTLY REACH FOR.
IMAGINE RUSSIAN DOLLAS - EACH LIST CAN CONTAIN ANOTHER LIST INSIDE IT. TO REACH THE INNERMOST DOLL, YOU NEED MULTIPLE [[ ]] OPERATIONS, LIKE OPENING EACH LAYER ONE BY ONE.

analogies.analogy: VECTORS ARE LIKE GROCERY BAGS WHERE ALL ITEMS MUST BE THE SAME (ALL FRUIT), WHILE LISTS ARE LIKE A HOUSE WHERE EACH ROOM CAN HOLD COMPLETELY DIFFERENT THINGS - KITCHEN HAS FOOD, BEDROOM HAS FURNITURE, OFFICE HAS ELECTRONICS.

LAPPLY IS LIKE HAVING A STAMP WITH A MESSAGE AND STAMPING IT ON EACH DOCUMENT IN A FILING CABINET. EACH DOCUMENT GETS THE SAME TREATMENT, AND YOU GET BACK ALL THE STAMPED DOCUMENTS (STILL IN CABINET/ LIST FORM).

A DATA FRAME IS LIKE A SPREADSHEET WHERE EACH COLUMN IS A LIST ELEMENT, BUT ALL COLUMNS MUST HAVE THE SAME NUMBER OF ROWS (LIKE ALL DRAWERS IN A DRESSER MUST BE SAME HEIGHT). THE SPREADSHEET IS A LIST WITH CONSTRAINTS.
UNLIST IS LIKE TAKING EVERYTHING OUT OF ALL THE FILES IN A CABINET AND THROWING IT ALL INTO ONE BIG PILE - YOU GET A SINGLE COLLECTION (VECTOR), BUT YOU LOSE THE ORGANIZATION (THE FILE STRUCTURE).
